summary: Verified StrictMode duplicate-POST fix in AuthCallback. All 3 targeted scenarios pass: (1) invalid session_id fires exactly 1 POST /api/auth/session under StrictMode and redirects to /?auth_error=..., (2) valid session cookie renders Dashboard cleanly, (3) backend logs Emergent 404 response body on invalid session_id.
critical_code_review_comments: Module-level `inFlight` flag never resets on unmount — acceptable for one-shot callback but note it prevents any legitimate retry within the same tab session; if user reloads /dashboard#session_id=... in the same SPA session, second attempt would be a no-op (falls through to loading spinner forever). Not a real user flow (Emergent redirects to a fresh session_id each time), so low risk.
server.py line 195: db.user_sessions.insert_one is called even if a session for this token already exists — no upsert / dedupe. Could accumulate duplicate session rows on retries. Minor.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AuthCallback duplicate-POST bug is fixed via module-scoped inFlight guard. Playwright confirms exactly 1 POST /api/auth/session under StrictMode for both success and error paths. Backend Emergent-response logging verified in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log.
rca of the issue: React StrictMode double-mounted AuthCallback in dev, causing useEffect to run twice and POST the one-time Emergent session_id twice. Second call returned 401 (user_data_not_found), overwriting the first call's /dashboard redirect with a redirect to /. Fix: module-level `let inFlight = false;` guard outside the component, checked/set at effect start, survives StrictMode remount so only the first mount performs the exchange. Hard window.location.replace() on both success and failure paths avoids router timing issues.
